pharma.title: India launches clinical study integrating traditional Ayurveda with modern TB treatments
pharma.summary: India has launched a multicentre clinical study that combines traditional Ayurveda interventions with standard tuberculosis treatment pathways. The programme adds a fresh evidence-building layer around TB care and opens up new communication needs across therapy education, physician engagement, and patient support.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's HCP digital and omnichannel engagement, patient education, and content management workflows to support evidence-led therapy communication as new TB study programmes unfold.

pharma.title: No shortage of anti-rabies vaccine, rabies immunoglobulin in India: Nadda
pharma.summary: The government said India has adequate anti-rabies vaccine and rabies immunoglobulin availability, backed by real-time supply tracking across the system. The update keeps vaccine and biologics access in focus and highlights the coordination needed between public-health messaging, supply visibility, and provider readiness.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's Doctor 360, HCP omnichannel engagement, and compliant content workflows to support vaccine-access communication, provider education, and coordinated field execution.

pharma.title: US-based Warburg Pincus in talks to buy Maneesh Pharma unit in Rs 1,800 crore deal
pharma.summary: Warburg Pincus is nearing a deal to buy the non-tuberculosis drug business of Maneesh Pharmaceuticals in a transaction valued at roughly Rs 1,600 crore to Rs 1,800 crore. The move signals sustained private-equity appetite for scale pharma assets in India even as portfolio consolidation continues across therapeutic categories.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's HCP omnichannel engagement, Doctor 360, and sales productivity workflows to support account prioritisation, launch planning, and compliant field execution as portfolio ownership changes.

pharma.title: Rising costs of key chemicals choke production at drug ingredient makers
pharma.summary: Indian bulk-drug manufacturers are facing cost pressure as solvent and intermediate prices rise sharply amid West Asia-linked supply disruption. The escalation is squeezing API margins and highlights the operational vulnerability of pharma supply chains to geopolitical shocks and freight volatility.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's Rep GPT, omnichannel engagement, and analytics workflows to help pharma teams respond faster to supply risk, portfolio shifts, and downstream prescriber communication needs.

pharma.title: India tightens grip on GLP-1 supply chain amid misuse concerns, expands crackdown on unapproved drugs
pharma.summary: India's drug regulator has intensified surveillance of GLP-1 weight-loss drug distribution amid concerns around unauthorised sales and misuse. The move broadens a parallel crackdown on unapproved medicines and raises the compliance bar for pharma companies operating in high-demand therapy categories.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's HCP omnichannel engagement, Rep GPT, Doctor 360, and compliant content management workflows to support responsible therapy education and more controlled field communication.

pharma.title: India's drug regulator flags 90 unapproved FDCs, directs states to take action
pharma.summary: India's drug regulator has flagged at least 90 unapproved fixed-dose combination drugs and directed states to act against their manufacturers, marketers, and distributors. The enforcement step underscores rising regulatory scrutiny on portfolio quality, approvals, and downstream market compliance.
pharma.thbAction: Position THB's HCP digital engagement, rep nudges, and content governance capabilities to help pharma teams communicate approved use, quality messaging, and compliance updates more effectively.

providers.title: KIMS Hospitals performs NitrX medial pivot knee replacement
providers.summary: KIMS Hospitals has completed a NitrX medial pivot knee replacement procedure, underscoring continued investment in advanced orthopaedic capability and specialised surgical care. The milestone highlights how hospitals are differentiating through high-acuity service lines, surgical expertise, and clinical programme visibility.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's hospital CRM, care coordinator workflows, and clinical intelligence layer to support service-line growth, referral capture, and post-procedure patient retention for high-acuity programmes.

providers.title: QCIL signs MoU with Intuitive to expand robotic-assisted surgery in tier 2 and 3 markets
providers.summary: Quality Care India has signed an MoU with Intuitive to scale robotic-assisted surgery across CARE Hospitals, KIMSHEALTH, and Evercare Hospitals through a phased rollout. The programme is aimed at extending advanced minimally invasive surgery into tier 2 and tier 3 markets while standardising capability across the network.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's digital front door, operational pathways, and patient retention workflows to support surgical programme growth, multi-facility coordination, and referral conversion as advanced services expand beyond metros.

providers.title: Delhi budget 2026: Big boost for health; critical, child care in focus
providers.summary: Delhi's new budget expands funding for critical care infrastructure, neonatal screening, hospital upgrades, ICU capacity, and primary healthcare access. The package highlights how public-sector investment is being redirected toward operational readiness, paediatric care, diagnostics, and bed-capacity visibility.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's digital front door, hospital CRM, and analytics suite to help providers manage service expansion, critical-care access visibility, and patient communication across newly funded facilities.

providers.title: RGHS cashless meds may stop from Mar 25
providers.summary: Private hospitals and pharmacy units under the Rajasthan Government Health Scheme warned that cashless medicine distribution may be suspended because reimbursements have been pending for seven to nine months. The standoff highlights how payment delays can disrupt medicine access, beneficiary communication, and continuity of hospital-linked outpatient services.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's hospital CRM, patient communication workflows, and analytics suite to manage beneficiary outreach, service continuity messaging, and escalation tracking when payment or access disruptions affect provider networks.

providers.title: Emergency units cry for toilets at govt hosps in Kol
providers.summary: A Times of India survey found major Kolkata government hospitals still lack accessible toilets within emergency wards, forcing critically ill patients and attendants to rely on distant paid facilities. The report underscores a persistent operations and patient-experience gap in emergency care infrastructure across high-volume public hospitals.
providers.thbAction: Align THB's operational pathways, care coordination workflows, and patient experience analytics to surface service bottlenecks early and support corrective action across emergency and high-volume care units.

providers.title: New critical care block at MIMS to provide specialised care
providers.summary: Mandya Institute of Medical Sciences is adding a 50-bed critical care block with ICUs, isolation wards, operating theatres, oxygen systems, and dialysis wards to improve emergency and specialty care. The expansion is designed to reduce tertiary transfers and strengthen round-the-clock acute care capacity for the district and nearby regions.
providers.thbAction: Align THB's care coordinator CRM, operational pathways, and clinical intelligence layer to support critical-care referrals, discharge follow-up, and service-line growth for expanding hospital systems.

providers.title: No Sunday care at Chennai UPHC after maternity hospital is razed
providers.summary: Residents in Kodambakkam have lost immediate public healthcare access on Sundays after a civic maternity hospital was demolished and the nearest urban primary health centre remains shut for one day each week. The gap highlights how infrastructure delays and limited operating hours can quickly affect access, antenatal support, and emergency response.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's digital front door, patient retention journeys, and care-gap workflows to help providers sustain access visibility, appointment continuity, and outreach when community facilities or service hours change.

providers.title: Ayushman Bharat cardiac care halted for 9 months at Gurgaon's Civil Hospital, probe underway
providers.summary: Cardiac treatment under Ayushman Bharat has been suspended for months at Gurgaon's Civil Hospital amid disputes over dues, registration, and billing practices in a PPP-run unit. The disruption raises patient-access concerns and spotlights the operational controls needed to manage scheme compliance and specialty service continuity.
providers.thbAction: Align THB's hospital CRM, contact-center workflows, and operational analytics to improve beneficiary communication, referral continuity, and escalation visibility when scheme-linked services face disruption.

providers.title: 360 ONE to finance AIMS founder's stake buyback
providers.summary: 360 ONE Asset is set to back AIMS founder Narendra Pandey as he seeks to buy back the 49 per cent stake held by private equity investors in the Faridabad hospital. The transaction signals continued investor interest in hospital ownership restructuring and capacity-led provider consolidation in India.
providers.thbAction: Position THB's hospital CRM, digital front door, and analytics stack to strengthen patient retention, referral visibility, and growth planning as provider networks change ownership or scale operations.

diagnostics.title: Affordable NPOC tests help expand TB testing to more people
diagnostics.summary: Express Healthcare reports that WHO-backed near-point-of-care molecular testing and easier sample collection could widen tuberculosis detection in frontline settings. The shift strengthens the case for lab networks that can support distributed testing, referral coordination, and high-volume public health screening programmes.
diagnostics.thbAction: Position THB's diagnostics CRM, disease management nudging, and analytics suite to improve screening uptake, doctor outreach, and referral conversion across TB programmes.

diagnostics.title: Alcon's broader commitment is helping people see brilliantly
diagnostics.summary: Alcon's India leadership says advanced ophthalmology platforms are reaching more cities beyond major metros, with growing adoption in cataract and vitreoretinal surgery. The trend points to expanding demand for specialist diagnostics workflows, installation support, and provider network engagement across eye-care ecosystems.
diagnostics.thbAction: Position THB's diagnostics CRM, analytics suite, and referral orchestration workflows to support specialist outreach, centre growth, and follow-up conversion across eye-care networks.

diagnostics.title: Silent epidemic: 60% CKD patients unaware of their condition, finds study
diagnostics.summary: A pilot study in Andhra Pradesh found that nearly 60 per cent of screened individuals had chronic kidney disease, while awareness remained extremely low in both urban and rural cohorts. The findings support broader CKD screening using point-of-care diagnostics and digital workflows to improve early detection and referral outcomes.
diagnostics.thbAction: Position THB's diagnostics CRM, disease management nudging, and analytics suite to drive screening conversion, follow-up compliance, and referral orchestration for chronic disease testing programmes.
